opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I have this non-stop, tickly dry cough that gets worse as soon as I lie down, and it's keeping me awake all night.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been going on for a few weeks now and I'm exhausted from not sleeping — I need to work out what's causing it and get some relief.

symptom_narrative.character: dry, hacking, tickly cough — a scratching feeling in the back of my throat
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bad enough that I can't sleep and I'm getting worn out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: feels like it's coming from the back of my throat
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse at night, especially when I lie down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — I've tried cough syrup from the chemist and it hasn't helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down flat makes it much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no phlegm or mucus that I've noticed, just the dry cough
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm exhausted from lack of sleep, it's really wearing me down

past_medical_history.how_managed: started a new tablet for it about a month ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: living alone, worried about being so tired overnight in case I trip or fall

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants a quick explanation and something to make the cough stop so he can sleep

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes the cough and its impact on sleep directly, sounds fatigued and frustrated

access_barriers.transport: lives alone, no mention of transport difficulty
